summary: Split the large ftCommon assembly range beginning at ftCo_800B3E04 out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C translation unit src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0B3E.c plus header, while preserving matching/functional behavior through inline ASM, jump-table/data reconstruction, and build-flag plumbing. The PR also adjusted Makefile MUST_MATCH/WIP defines for both C and assembly, registered the new object, moved/deleted ft_08A4 header declarations, added small includes/prototypes in neighboring ftCommon files, and updated progress/reporting helper scripts.
what_changed: Removed roughly 9.6k lines of ftCo_0B3E-related assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced that content with a new 9.4k-line C file at src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0B3E.c.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0B3E.h and removed obsolete declarations from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, indicating the split now owns its own ftCommon-local interface.

The new C file contains MWERKS_GEKKO-only data definitions for doubles/floats, strings such as "csP is bad address\n" and "ftcmdscript.c", force_active data, many local label forward declarations, and jtbl_t jump tables such as ftCo_803C5DD4, ftCo_803C6118, ftCo_803C613C, and ftCo_803C61BC.
Functions in the split include inline ASM guarded by MUST_MATCH/WIP logic; the excerpt shows ftCo_800B3E04 using an ASM function body with a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub in non-matching/WIP configurations.

Makefile was changed so ASFLAGS receives --defsym MUST_MATCH=1 inside the same non-NON_MATCHING block that defines CFLAGS -DMUST_MATCH, and WIP now also adds --defsym WIP=1 for assembly in addition to CFLAGS -DWIP.
obj_files.mk gained the new ftCo_0B3E object so the new translation unit participates in the build.

Neighboring ftCommon files ftCo_09F7.c and ftCo_0A01.c gained small additions, and ftCo_0A01.h lost one line, likely to satisfy new cross-file references after the split; the context does not show exact symbols.
src/melee/mp/mplib.h and src/macros.inc received small support edits, likely for declarations/macros required by the split, but the excerpt does not provide exact content.

Tooling changed alongside the split: tools/report_score.py, tools/merge_double.py, tools/calcprogress/src/find_asm_functions.py, and requirements.txt were updated, suggesting the split exposed reporting/progress or double-constant handling needs.
smart_moves: Kept the split buildable before full C decompilation by moving original assembly into ASM blocks inside the new C file rather than requiring immediate high-level matches for every large function.
Preserved local control-flow/data dependencies by explicitly declaring label targets and reconstructing jump tables as jtbl_t arrays in C, rather than leaving them stranded in the old assembly file.
Used MWERKS_GEKKO guards around compiler-specific data/constant definitions so host/tool builds are less likely to choke on target-only layout artifacts.

Used #pragma force_active for data that must stay emitted, including a string with suspicious trailing NUL padding noted by a TODO, which is a useful tactic for preserving data layout while uncertainty remains.
Updated ASFLAGS and CFLAGS together for MUST_MATCH and WIP so inline assembly and standalone assembly see the same preprocessor/build-mode intent.
Registered the new object and created a dedicated header, reducing the size and responsibility of the generic ft_08A4 assembly/header bucket.
decomp_lessons: Large assembly ranges can be split into a C translation unit before all functions are high-level C by embedding ASM bodies under build-mode guards; this gives progress accounting and better file ownership without blocking on full decompilation.
When moving assembly into C, data layout matters as much as text: constants, strings, sdata externs, force_active regions, and jump tables must move with the code that references them.
For switch/jump-table-heavy code, keep anonymous/local labels addressable by forward-declaring label functions and building jtbl_t arrays with the exact label order and repeated default targets.
Build-mode logic must be checked for both C compiler defines and assembler defsyms; a split that mixes C and assembly can fail subtly if MUST_MATCH or WIP is only visible to one side.

A WIP configuration may intentionally choose stubs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED even when matching assembly exists, while a non-WIP non-matching target may prefer original asm for functionality; this distinction was explicitly discussed in review.
Leave uncertainty documented in-place for data oddities, such as suspected string padding/split boundaries, rather than silently normalizing them during a mechanical split.
naming_conventions: New split file and header use the ftCommon address-range style: ftCo_0B3E.c and ftCo_0B3E.h.
Symbols in the new file use the ftCo_ prefix plus address, e.g. ftCo_800B3E04, ftCo_800B77E8, ftCo_803C5DD4, and ftCo_804D89C8.
Unidentified local branch targets remain address labels with lbl_ prefixes, e.g. lbl_800B3EDC, lbl_800B45FC, lbl_800B6414, and lbl_800B8840.
Data labels preserve address-based names grouped by section/address, such as ftCo_803C5DB0 for strings/data and ftCo_804D89C8 for floating constants.
The split moved declarations out of the broad ft_08A4.h namespace toward a dedicated ftCo_0B3E.h interface.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline ASM function pattern shown: `ASM void ftCo_800B3E04(...)` with `nofralloc` body for MUST_MATCH and a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub when `!defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)`.
Assembler build flags now mirror C build flags: MUST_MATCH becomes both `--defsym MUST_MATCH=1` and `-DMUST_MATCH`; WIP becomes both `--defsym WIP=1` and `-DWIP`.
Jump tables were expressed as `jtbl_t` arrays containing label function pointers, including repeated fallback/default labels such as lbl_800B45FC and lbl_800B8840.
Compiler-specific constants are guarded with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, including doubles using values such as `S32_TO_F32`, trigonometric constants, and exact float/double literals.
`#pragma push`, `#pragma force_active on`, and `#pragma pop` were used to force emission of data strings/padding required for matching.

The PR added/updated tooling such as merge_double.py and report_score.py, implying double constant handling and scoring/reporting needed support for this style of split, but exact algorithms are not present in the excerpt.
review_feedback: Reviewer r-burns questioned the interaction of MUST_MATCH and WIP in ftCo_0B3E.c: defining MUST_MATCH appeared not to always result in matching code if WIP was also defined.

The author response, as excerpted, explained that WIP is used for cases such as `todo unused stack`, and that `MWERKS_GEKKO && !MUST_MATCH && !WIP` should use original asm because that build is intended to be non-matching but as functional as possible, whereas WIP functions may be incomplete.
No other substantive review requests are visible in the provided context; issue comments were zero and only two review comments were present.
follow_up_queries: How did tools/merge_double.py change in PR #913, and was it introduced specifically for extracting double constants from assembly splits?
What exact declarations were moved from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h into src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0B3E.h?
What small support changes were made to src/macros.inc and src/melee/mp/mplib.h for this split?
Did later PRs replace the inline ASM bodies in ftCo_0B3E.c with high-level C, especially ftCo_800B3E04, ftCo_800B77E8, and ftCo_800B683C?
Was the TODO about `command script buffer over flow!\n\0\0\0\0` later resolved as padding or a separate split?
Did the MUST_MATCH/WIP build-mode convention established here remain stable in later decomp splits?
